# Database & Migrations Instructions

**Scope:** `supabase/migrations/**`, database schema, RLS policies

Use this guide when making database schema changes, creating migrations, and managing data isolation.

---

## 📋 Mandatory Reading

**Before making database changes, read:**
1. [ARCHITECTURE.md](../../documentation/ARCHITECTURE.md) — Multi-tenancy design, RLS policies
2. Migration files: `supabase/migrations/0001-0026*.sql` — Existing patterns

---

## 🎯 Critical Rules

### Rule 1: Every Table Needs RLS Policies

**If you create a new table, you MUST add RLS policies to enforce tenant isolation.**

```sql
-- ✅ ALWAYS ADD THESE POLICIES

-- Enable RLS on the table
ALTER TABLE my_table ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Tenant admins can select/insert/update/delete their tenant's data
CREATE POLICY "Tenant can manage own data" ON my_table
  USING (
    auth.uid() IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM memberships 
      WHERE tenant_id = my_table.tenant_id
    )
  )
  WITH CHECK (
    auth.uid() IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM memberships 
      WHERE tenant_id = my_table.tenant_id
    )
  );

-- Super admins (service role) bypass RLS automatically
-- No policy needed for service role
```

**Without RLS:**
- ❌ One tenant can see/modify another tenant's data
- ❌ Security breach
- ❌ Will be caught in code review

**Rule:** RLS is enforced at the database layer. Always use it.

---

### Rule 2: Migrations Are Immutable & Numbered

**Migrations run once in order. Never edit old migrations.**

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT APPROACH
supabase/migrations/0001_create_tenants.sql       # Old, shipped
supabase/migrations/0002_create_memberships.sql   # Old, shipped
supabase/migrations/0027_add_new_column.sql       # NEW migration

# ❌ WRONG APPROACH
supabase/migrations/0001_create_tenants.sql       # ← Don't modify!
supabase/migrations/0002_create_memberships.sql   # ← Don't modify!
```

**Why:** If you edit old migrations:
1. They won't run again (Supabase tracks which have executed)
2. Production database won't get the change
3. Local and prod become out of sync

**Rule:** Always create a NEW migration file for changes.

---

### Rule 3: Test Migrations Locally First

```bash
# Reset local DB and run all migrations
pnpm run db:reset

# If successful, then apply to cloud
pnpm run db:migrate
```

**Important:** `db:reset` **deletes all data**. Only use in development.

```bash
# Apply to production Supabase (when ready)
pnpm run db:migrate
```

---

### Rule 4: After Schema Changes, Regenerate Types

```bash
# Run this after EVERY migration
pnpm run db:types
```

This generates `packages/lib/supabase/types.ts` so frontend code has correct type safety.

```bash
# Example workflow
1. Create migration:     supabase/migrations/0027_add_feature.sql
2. Test locally:         pnpm run db:reset
3. Regenerate types:     pnpm run db:types
4. Deploy to prod:       pnpm run db:migrate
5. Verify in app:        pnpm dev
```

---

### Rule 5: Follow Naming Conventions

**Migrations:**
```sql
-- Format: NNNN_description_of_change.sql
0001_create_tenants.sql
0027_add_media_page_associations.sql
0028_add_soft_delete_column.sql
```

**Tables:**
```sql
-- snake_case, plural form
CREATE TABLE pages (...)
CREATE TABLE media_page_associations (...)
CREATE TABLE feature_flags (...)
```

**Columns:**
```sql
-- snake_case
id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY
tenant_id BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id)
created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
```

---

## 📋 Migration Template

Use this template for new migrations:

```sql
-- Migration: NNNN_description
-- Description: What this change does
-- RLS: [Yes/No] - whether new tables have RLS

-- Create table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS new_table (
  id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY,
  tenant_id BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  
  -- Multi-column unique constraint (optional)
  UNIQUE(tenant_id, name)
);

-- Create indexes (for performance)
CREATE INDEX idx_new_table_tenant_id ON new_table(tenant_id);

-- Enable RLS
ALTER TABLE new_table ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- RLS Policy 1: Tenant admins can view
CREATE POLICY "Tenant can view own" ON new_table
  FOR SELECT
  USING (
    auth.uid() IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM memberships 
      WHERE tenant_id = new_table.tenant_id
    )
  );

-- RLS Policy 2: Tenant admins can insert
CREATE POLICY "Tenant can insert own" ON new_table
  FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (
    auth.uid() IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM memberships 
      WHERE tenant_id = new_table.tenant_id
    )
  );

-- RLS Policy 3: Tenant admins can update
CREATE POLICY "Tenant can update own" ON new_table
  FOR UPDATE
  USING (
    auth.uid() IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM memberships 
      WHERE tenant_id = new_table.tenant_id
    )
  )
  WITH CHECK (
    auth.uid() IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM memberships 
      WHERE tenant_id = new_table.tenant_id
    )
  );

-- RLS Policy 4: Tenant admins can delete
CREATE POLICY "Tenant can delete own" ON new_table
  FOR DELETE
  USING (
    auth.uid() IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM memberships 
      WHERE tenant_id = new_table.tenant_id
    )
  );

-- Optional: Super admin bypass (service role automatically bypasses RLS)
-- No additional policy needed
```

---

## 🔒 RLS Policy Patterns

### Pattern 1: Tenant-Based Access (Most Common)
```sql
-- User must be a member of the tenant
CREATE POLICY "Tenant can manage own" ON pages
  USING (
    auth.uid() IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM memberships 
      WHERE tenant_id = pages.tenant_id
    )
  );
```

### Pattern 2: Specific Role-Based Access
```sql
-- User must be a platform admin
CREATE POLICY "Platform admins only" ON platform_admins
  USING (
    auth.uid() IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM platform_admins
    )
  );
```

### Pattern 3: Public Read-Only
```sql
-- Anyone can read published pages
CREATE POLICY "Published pages are public" ON pages
  FOR SELECT
  USING (is_published = true);
```

### Pattern 4: User-Specific Access
```sql
-- User can only access their own data
CREATE POLICY "Users see own data" ON user_settings
  USING (user_id = auth.uid());
```

---

## 🏗️ Existing Table Schema

**Key tables to know about:**

### tenants
```sql
id, slug, domain, custom_domain, created_at
-- One per SaaS customer
```

### memberships
```sql
id, tenant_id, user_id, role, created_at
-- Maps users to tenants (role = 'admin' | 'editor' | 'viewer')
```

### pages
```sql
id, tenant_id, title, slug, is_published, is_homepage, sections, blocks, created_at
```

### sections & blocks
```sql
id, page_id, type, content_json, order, created_at
```

### media
```sql
id, tenant_id, filename, size, mime_type, metadata, created_at
```

### media_page_associations
```sql
id, media_id, page_id, usage_type, display_mode, created_at
-- Links media to pages (usage_type = 'hero' | 'gallery' | 'thumbnail')
```

### subscriptions
```sql
id, tenant_id, plan, stripe_customer_id, stripe_subscription_id, created_at
```

### feature_flags
```sql
id, tenant_id, feature_key, is_enabled, created_at
-- Overrides plan defaults per-tenant
```

---

## 🚫 Anti-Patterns (NEVER Do These)

| Anti-Pattern | Why | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Edit old migrations | Won't re-run, breaks sync | Create new migration |
| Skip RLS policies | Data leaks across tenants | Add RLS to every table |
| Don't test locally | Migrations fail in production | Always run `db:reset` first |
| Forget `pnpm run db:types` | Types out of sync | Regenerate types after migration |
| Hardcode user IDs | Breaks multi-tenancy | Use `auth.uid()` + membership check |
| Cascade deletes without care | Data loss across tables | Use `ON DELETE CASCADE` or `RESTRICT` |
| Create indexes on everything | Slow inserts, bloated DB | Index columns used in WHERE/JOIN |

---

## 📋 Checklist: Making a Database Change

- [ ] Created new migration file: `supabase/migrations/NNNN_description.sql`
- [ ] Migration includes RLS policies (if creating new table)
- [ ] Migration tested locally: `pnpm run db:reset`
- [ ] Migration applied: `pnpm run db:migrate`
- [ ] Types regenerated: `pnpm run db:types`
- [ ] Changes committed (both migration + types)
- [ ] Verified in app: `pnpm dev` (check for type errors)

---

## 🔄 Common Tasks

### Add a New Column to Existing Table
```sql
-- Migration: 0027_add_feature_column.sql

ALTER TABLE pages ADD COLUMN feature_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;

-- Create index if queried frequently
CREATE INDEX idx_pages_feature_enabled ON pages(feature_enabled);
```

### Create a New Table
```sql
-- Use template above - always include RLS policies
```

### Drop a Column (with Caution)
```sql
-- Migration: 0028_drop_old_column.sql

-- Check for dependencies first
ALTER TABLE pages DROP COLUMN old_field;
```

### Rename a Column
```sql
-- Migration: 0029_rename_column.sql

ALTER TABLE pages RENAME COLUMN old_name TO new_name;
```

### Update Existing Data
```sql
-- Migration: 0030_backfill_data.sql

UPDATE pages 
SET status = 'draft' 
WHERE status IS NULL;
```
